title: Cross-Device Handoff Envelope
description: Canonical envelope for handing off runtime session state between Kyberion-managed surfaces (web browser, iOS WebView, Android WebView). The envelope is what export_session_handoff produces and import_session_handoff consumes. The schema is intentionally minimal — surface-specific details live under `surface_state` and are validated by the originating actuator.

envelope_version.description: Schema version; bump when breaking the envelope shape.

handoff_id.description: Unique handoff identifier; emitted in audit-chain on both export and import.

issued_at.description: When the source surface produced the envelope.

expires_at.description: Beyond this time the envelope must be rejected by importers; bound the credential exposure window.

mission_ref.description: The mission whose state is being handed off; importer attaches the imported envelope as evidence on the same mission.

principal.description: Identity that authorized the handoff. Required when handing off authenticated session state.

surface_state.description: Surface-specific opaque payload. Validated by the originating actuator's own schema. Importers MUST refuse if `surface_state.contract_ref` does not match a known contract.

contract_ref.description: URI to the actuator-specific schema, e.g. browser-actuator.session-handoff.v1.

secret_refs.description: Pointers to secrets that the importer must resolve via SecretResolver. Secret values themselves MUST NOT live in the envelope.

max_replay_count.description: Reject the envelope after this many imports; protects against replay.

allowed_target_origins.description: Importer MUST refuse if the active origin is not in this list.

audit.description: Hash-chain anchors so the importer can verify continuity with the source surface.

source_chain_tip.description: audit-chain tip on the source side at issuance time.

expected_target_chain_parent.description: If set, importer must append with this as parent_hash, i.e. confirm continuity.

id.description: Stable identifier for the surface instance (browser_session_id, app_install_id, etc.).

user_agent_hint.description: Loose UA / device hint for routing; do not authorize on this.
